title: Sugar Mama

jokes.text: It's a full moon. You know what that means. Every creep in this city will be out tonight.
jokes.setup: Louie warns about the full moon bringing out crazies
jokes.punchline: Sets up the episode's theme while establishing Louie's cynical worldview
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that sets up episode theme, but fairly standard cynical dispatcher line

jokes.text: Well, be sure to wear a jacket, Louie, it's getting chilly.
jokes.setup: Louie warns about creeps being out
jokes.punchline: Rieger implies Louie himself is one of the creeps
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback with good economy - Rieger turns Louie's warning back on him

jokes.text: You think you're a real Jan Murray, don't you, Rieger?
jokes.setup: Rieger makes a witty comeback
jokes.punchline: Louie references obscure 1960s comedian as an insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - using dated reference as an insult shows his age and personality

jokes.text: Shea stadium, please. / There's no game tonight.
jokes.setup: Customer requests ride to stadium
jokes.punchline: Driver points out there's no game, questioning her logic
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup for eccentric customer character

jokes.text: I didn't ask for the schedule, thank you.
jokes.setup: Alex questions why she wants to go to stadium with no game
jokes.punchline: Dee's matter-of-fact dismissal of logical concern
jokes.explanation: Great economy and character establishment - Dee's polite but firm dismissal of logic

jokes.text: Old Ebbets field looked good even in the dark. / Lady, I didn't build the place, I just... / Well, never mind, never mind.
jokes.setup: Dee criticizes Shea Stadium architecture
jokes.punchline: Alex starts to defend himself then gives up mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Alex realizes arguing with elderly customer is futile

jokes.text: I know a place at the end of one the runways where the big ones come down so close the lights shine right in the window and you can hear them lower their landing gear
jokes.setup: Dee changes destination to airport
jokes.punchline: Her detailed knowledge of specific plane-watching spot reveals eccentric hobby
jokes.explanation: Excellent character detail - specific, vivid description makes her eccentricity feel real and endearing

jokes.text: Hey, full moon...
jokes.setup: Louie's earlier warning about full moon crazies
jokes.punchline: Alex realizes he's gotten one of the crazies Louie warned about
jokes.explanation: Good callback that connects earlier setup to current situation with nice economy

jokes.text: That's the closest one yet, Alex. / Yeah, I think he left some rubber on the roof.
jokes.setup: They're watching planes land at close range
jokes.punchline: Plane came so close it might have touched the taxi roof
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy implied by dialogue - escalates the absurdity of their plane watching

jokes.text: Do you do this often? / Oop! That was a question, I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Dee mentioned she likes drivers who don't talk or ask questions
jokes.punchline: Alex immediately asks question then catches himself
jokes.explanation: Nice self-aware moment - Alex catches himself violating her stated preference immediately

jokes.text: You should have used the express line.
jokes.setup: Dee says she was at Woolworth's for 35 years
jokes.punchline: Alex treats it like she was waiting in a checkout line
jokes.explanation: Excellent one-liner with perfect economy - clever misinterpretation of her statement

jokes.text: Got you that time, huh?
jokes.setup: Alex delivers the express line joke
jokes.punchline: He's pleased with his own comedic success
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Alex's satisfaction with his joke, though not particularly clever itself

jokes.text: Everything is on our way.
jokes.setup: Dee asks if Woolworth's is on their way
jokes.punchline: Alex's philosophical take on taxi driving
jokes.explanation: Nice insight into taxi driver mentality - philosophical but practical

jokes.text: On a Woolworth's pension?
jokes.setup: Dee says she's 'filthy comfortable' financially
jokes.punchline: Alex's skeptical reaction to her claimed wealth
jokes.explanation: Good realistic reaction - Alex can't believe Woolworth's clerk could be wealthy

jokes.text: There's a moral in there somewhere.
jokes.setup: Dee's story about brief marriage to wealthy man who died
jokes.punchline: Her wry acknowledgment of the questionable morality
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment - Dee acknowledges the moral ambiguity of her fortune

jokes.text: Congratulations, Alex, you set a new record. / Well, thank you. I couldn't have done it without you.
jokes.setup: Dee gives him $26, beating his previous best tip of $25
jokes.punchline: Alex treats it like an achievement award ceremony
jokes.explanation: Nice play on award ceremony language for such a small achievement

jokes.text: Well, thank you. I couldn't have done it without you.
jokes.setup: Dee's mock congratulations
jokes.punchline: Alex plays along with the absurd achievement celebration
jokes.explanation: Good comedic chemistry. Alex matches Dee's playful energy.

jokes.text: You see, it's like this: I don't give a rat's corpse whether you get a clean cab or not.
jokes.setup: Driver asks Louie to explain why he always gets dirty cabs
jokes.punchline: Louie's brutally honest and colorful explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - sets up helpful explanation then delivers brutal honesty with colorful language

jokes.text: Oh... okay. Thanks for explaining it to me. / I enjoyed it.
jokes.setup: Driver asked for explanation, got brutal honesty
jokes.punchline: Driver's polite acceptance and Louie's satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Good cringe follow-up - driver's resignation and Louie's sadistic pleasure

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, and one guy who thought I was the most beautiful woman in the world.
jokes.setup: Tony lists his crazy passengers including drunks, flasher, transvestites
jokes.punchline: The most absurd one - someone thought Tony was beautiful woman
jokes.explanation: Good escalating punchline - saves most absurd passenger for last

jokes.text: For 26 bucks I'd drive around and sing 'You Light Up My Life.'
jokes.setup: Alex mentions getting $26 tip for driving around and talking
jokes.punchline: Tony would do something even more embarrassing for that money
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation joke referencing contemporary cheesy hit song

jokes.text: I know. I'm eating. / So you are. / No... I mean, I'm eating.
jokes.setup: Jim says he's eating in response to Tony's singing comment
jokes.punchline: Confused exchange about literal vs. figurative 'eating'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on slang meaning of 'eating it' vs. literally eating

jokes.text: I hope I am, because the only thing I can think about is the fact that my hair is growing all the time.
jokes.setup: Jim thinks he might still be high from contact high
jokes.punchline: His stoned observation about hair constantly growing
jokes.explanation: Good stoner humor - the kind of mundane observation that seems profound when high

jokes.text: I'd kill for a Twinkie.
jokes.setup: Jim is supposedly high from contact high
jokes.punchline: Classic stoner munchies craving
jokes.explanation: Standard stoner joke but good economy and timing

jokes.text: Alex, who am I spilling my guts out to?
jokes.setup: Elaine shares personal struggles with stranger Dee
jokes.punchline: She suddenly realizes she's oversharing to a stranger
jokes.explanation: Realistic moment of self-awareness about oversharing

jokes.text: Whoever brought her in, get her out.
jokes.setup: Dee is being friendly and social in the garage
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately wants this pleasant person removed
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie - his immediate hostility to someone being nice

jokes.text: I bet you're Louie. Am I right? / Sell it someplace else, sister. / I am right.
jokes.setup: Dee tries to charm Louie
jokes.punchline: Louie's hostility confirms his identity to her
jokes.explanation: Good character interaction - Louie's rudeness ironically confirms her guess

jokes.text: Old age is a funny thing. I woke up this morning, found myself wanting blue hair.
jokes.setup: Dee reflects on aging
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd desire that comes with age
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about elderly stereotypes delivered naturally

jokes.text: Well, don't rush into anything, Dee.
jokes.setup: Dee mentions wanting blue hair
jokes.punchline: Alex's mock-serious advice about hair color
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan response treating trivial decision as momentous

jokes.text: Pretty slick, huh?
jokes.setup: Dee tells story of how George gave her candy box meant for 'most beautiful girl'
jokes.punchline: Her proud assessment of his pickup technique
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Dee's admiration for simple romantic gesture

jokes.text: Well... I miss George's crapola.
jokes.setup: Sentimental discussion about missing her late husband
jokes.punchline: Dee's crude but affectionate term for what she misses
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - crude but endearing way to express missing someone

jokes.text: being poor was best left to people with no money.
jokes.setup: Dee describes learning to enjoy being rich
jokes.punchline: Her circular logic about poverty
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with philosophical undertones - memorable and quotable

jokes.text: Oh, now, Dee I am not going to take $100 from you. Now, look right at me. I'm not taking $100 from you. / Oh, please don't say no. Makes me feel patronized. / Okay. You win, I lose. So why don't I feel worse?
jokes.setup: Alex firmly refuses large tip
jokes.punchline: He immediately caves and questions his own principles
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex's moral flexibility when faced with money

jokes.text: Well, maybe money doesn't mean that much to me. What's important to me is what I can buy with my money: Nice food, nice clothes, nice people.
jokes.setup: Discussion about money's importance
jokes.punchline: Dee casually includes 'nice people' as something money can buy
jokes.explanation: Dark insight delivered casually - reveals Dee's transactional view of relationships

jokes.text: You know, Alex, when I first got this job, everyone told me that the best fares were in the upper 80s. I thought they were talking about streets.
jokes.setup: Tony mentions advice about best fares in 'upper 80s'
jokes.punchline: He misunderstood - they meant ages, not street numbers
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with clever double meaning

jokes.text: Customer... she's the mother lode.
jokes.setup: Alex calls Dee just a customer
jokes.punchline: John's mining metaphor for rich elderly client
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor for financial opportunity. Shows the drivers' opportunism.

jokes.text: Did Mommy Warbucks raise your allowance?
jokes.setup: Louie mocks Alex's relationship with wealthy Dee
jokes.punchline: Reference to Annie's benefactor Daddy Warbucks
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits the dynamic

jokes.text: That broad is older than the continental shelf!
jokes.setup: Louie is mocking Dee's age
jokes.punchline: Geological comparison for extreme age
jokes.explanation: Creative geological age comparison typical of Louie's colorful insults

jokes.text: Her age would make a good bowling score!
jokes.setup: Continuing age jokes
jokes.punchline: Age so high it's like bowling score (200+)
jokes.explanation: Less creative than the continental shelf line but maintains the escalation

jokes.text: Oh, Alex, be a dear and take the kitty out for a wee-wee.
jokes.setup: Louie mocking Alex's relationship with elderly Dee
jokes.punchline: Imitating stereotypical old lady speech
jokes.explanation: Standard elderly stereotype but delivered in Louie's mocking style

jokes.text: That's hot coffee!
jokes.setup: Someone offers Louie a drink to try
jokes.punchline: Louie burns himself on unexpectedly hot coffee
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy - Louie gets comeuppance for his mockery

jokes.text: Well, Happy Birthday, early or late, however you want to call it.
jokes.setup: Alex says he can only accept gifts if it's his birthday
jokes.punchline: Dee's flexible interpretation of birthday timing
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's determination to give gifts by any rationalization

jokes.text: My God, I know everybody here.
jokes.setup: Dee describes all her friends at the dance
jokes.punchline: Alex's realization about her detailed descriptions
jokes.explanation: Good realization moment about how much Dee has shared

jokes.text: Are you kidding? / Oh, dear. I mean... I mean the one with the white carnation in his lapel.
jokes.setup: Dee asks Alex to identify man with gray hair
jokes.punchline: Alex's response implies every man there has gray hair
jokes.explanation: Good visual joke about age demographics at senior dance

jokes.text: You may not see any smoke, but I happen to know there's a fire in that furnace.
jokes.setup: Dee describes her interest in Weldon
jokes.punchline: Her metaphor about his hidden passion
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor showing Dee's romantic optimism

jokes.text: I'll just be over here holding up the wall.
jokes.setup: Alex left alone as Dee goes to dance
jokes.punchline: His resigned acceptance of wallflower status
jokes.explanation: Standard wallflower expression but fits the moment

jokes.text: That's the only thing foxy about her.
jokes.setup: Ramon mentions his date with the fox stole
jokes.punchline: The stole is the only 'foxy' thing about her
jokes.explanation: Basic pun but establishes Ramon's character

jokes.text: Yeah. I wonder when the government will get around to socialized Maserati tune-ups?
jokes.setup: Ramon complains about Maserati maintenance costs
jokes.punchline: Alex's sarcastic response about government assistance
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about luxury problems masquerading as real hardship

jokes.text: Call me, uh, Tandalayo.
jokes.setup: Ramon introduces himself by his gigolo name
jokes.punchline: Alex responds with equally ridiculous fake name
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation matching Ramon's energy

jokes.text: You mean you're dating grandma for nothing?
jokes.setup: Alex explains he's not on the gigolo circuit
jokes.punchline: Ramon's incredulous reaction to unpaid companion work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ramon's transactional worldview

jokes.text: Thanks. I like your ribs.
jokes.setup: Ramon compliments Alex's tie
jokes.punchline: Alex's bizarre response complimenting body parts
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird non sequitur that shows Alex's discomfort

jokes.text: Uh, no, I won the Rhumba contest. I was carrying you.
jokes.setup: Dee claims they won the dance contest
jokes.punchline: Alex corrects the record about who carried whom
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Dee's pride with dance partnership reality

jokes.text: Wouldn't be the same without the money.
jokes.setup: Alex suggests ending the paid arrangement but staying friends
jokes.punchline: Dee's blunt admission that money is essential to the relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent character revelation - Dee's brutal honesty about transactional relationships

jokes.text: Well, next time you go into a restaurant you tell the Maitre d' if he gives you a good table, you'll be his buddy.
jokes.setup: Alex suggests friendship as alternative to money
jokes.punchline: Dee's practical example of why money works better
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cynical observation about money vs. friendship in service relationships

jokes.text: I know people gave their whole lives to their children. Those children don't even come to see them. If I had any kids, you can bet your buttons I would put them on my payroll, and they'd come to see me.
jokes.setup: Dee explains her philosophy about money in relationships
jokes.punchline: Her plan to pay children to visit her
jokes.explanation: Dark but logical extension of her money philosophy - brilliant character insight

jokes.text: Especially on payday.
jokes.setup: Dee's plan to pay children to visit
jokes.punchline: The cynical punchline about payday visits
jokes.explanation: Perfect cynical capper to her transactional family theory

jokes.text: Why do I sound like Joan Crawford?
jokes.setup: Alex delivers dramatic speech about feeling used
jokes.punchline: He realizes his own melodramatic delivery
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment deflating his own dramatic speech

jokes.text: Aw, don't stop, it was just starting to be fun.
jokes.setup: Alex's melodramatic speech about being used
jokes.punchline: Dee's enjoyment of the drama
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's entertainment value in conflict - treats drama like performance

jokes.text: Horse manure, you love me.
jokes.setup: Alex says he doesn't like her anymore
jokes.punchline: Dee's blunt dismissal of his claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee line - crude but insightful dismissal of Alex's protest

jokes.text: I just hope my stomach can take those dumps you can afford.
jokes.setup: Alex insists on paying for lunch
jokes.punchline: Dee's backhanded concern about restaurant quality
jokes.explanation: Good ending line showing Dee's class consciousness even in compromise

tmdb_overview: After a ride in Alex's cab, a widow decides to rent him out as her chauffeur.
insight: Early Taxi stumbles: 66 score with thin character work and zero standout punchlines.
